steps.title: Use the area relationship
steps.content: For a rectangle, area equals width multiplied by length. The expression $w(w+64)$ already shows the area as a product. Since the width is $w$, the other factor must represent the length.

steps.title: Divide by the width
steps.content: Let $L$ be the length. Then $$wL=w(w+64)$$ $$L=\frac{w(w+64)}{w}=w+64$$ The answer is C.
